sections.title: Markets & finance
sections.desc: Equities, rates, credit, the dollar, and the Fed.

sections.title: Economy
sections.desc: Inflation, jobs, growth, and the consumer.

sections.title: Debt & deficit
sections.desc: What Washington owes, borrows, and pays in interest.

sections.title: Housing
sections.desc: Prices, rents, rates, supply, and stress.

sections.title: Energy
sections.desc: Oil, gas, electricity, and U.S. production.

sections.title: Demographics, immigration & education
sections.desc: Who and what America is today, and how it has changed: population, its makeup, immigration, and schooling.

sections.title: Crime & justice
sections.desc: Violent and property crime, how many killings are solved, and incarceration.

sections.title: Society & politics
sections.desc: Fertility, life expectancy, overdoses, marriage, trust, religion.

sections.title: Tech & AI
sections.desc: AI progress, chips, and the buildout.

sections.title: World
sections.desc: China, geopolitics, and the external picture.

t10y2y.title: Yield curve (10Y minus 2Y)
t10y2y.note: Inversions (below zero) have preceded every modern recession.

hy_oas.title: High-yield credit spread (OAS)
hy_oas.note: Stress gauge: widening spreads = markets pricing more default risk. Note: FRED now serves only ~3 years of this ICE index; see 'Credit stress, long-run (Baa spread)' for the multi-decade view.

baa_spread.title: Credit stress, long-run (Baa spread)
baa_spread.note: The extra yield investors demand to hold medium-grade (Baa) corporate bonds over Treasuries -- the long-run companion to the high-yield spread (which FRED now caps at ~3 years). It widens in every recession. Daily since 1986.

wage_vs_cpi.title: Wages vs. inflation, YoY
wage_vs_cpi.note: Wages above CPI = real wages rising.

retail_yoy.title: Real retail sales, YoY
retail_yoy.note: Inflation-adjusted — nominal retail sales can grow while real volumes shrink.

deficit12.title: Federal deficit, trailing 12 months
deficit12.note: Positive = deficit. Monthly Treasury Statement, rolling 12-month sum.

inventory.title: Homes for sale: active listings
inventory.note: Inventory turns before prices do. Series begins 2016.

swb_fy.title: Southwest border apprehensions by fiscal year (USBP)
swb_fy.note: Curated from CBP published fiscal-year data; updated each fall when CBP closes the fiscal year.

murder_rate.title: Murder rate (per 100,000)
murder_rate.note: Curated from FBI annual estimates; updated when the FBI publishes each fall.

rtci_ytd.title: Crime YTD vs. last year (real-time city sample)
rtci_ytd.note: Sample of 300+ agencies reporting monthly; provisional, not official FBI totals.

overdoses.title: Drug overdose deaths (trailing 12 months)
overdoses.note: CDC publishes with about a 6-month lag; recent months use CDC's predicted (completeness-adjusted) counts.

fertility.title: Total fertility rate
fertility.note: Replacement level ≈ 2.1. Curated annual data (CDC final).

marriage.title: Marriage rate (per 1,000 population)
marriage.note: Curated annual data (provisional for recent years).

trust_media.title: Trust in mass media (Gallup)
trust_media.note: Curated from Gallup's annual survey (published each fall).

church.title: Church/synagogue/mosque membership (Gallup)
church.note: Gallup series; last reading 2020. A living replacement series is on the backlog.

ai_compute.title: AI training compute of notable models
ai_compute.note: Each point is a notable model (log scale).

ai_releases.title: Notable AI model releases per quarter

datacenter.title: Data center construction spending (monthly)
datacenter.source_name: Census C30 via Our World in Data

sahm.title: Sahm rule recession indicator
sahm.note: Rise of 0.50 pp in the 3-month-avg unemployment rate off its 12-month low; a reading ≥ 0.50 has marked the start of every recession since 1970.

ccsa.title: Continued jobless claims
ccsa.note: Rising continued claims = laid-off workers not finding new jobs.

quits.title: Quits rate (JOLTS)
quits.note: Workers quit when confident; the quits rate turns before payrolls do.

temphelp.title: Temp-help employment, YoY
temphelp.note: Temp staffing is cut first and hired first — it leads the broader labor market.

ppi_yoy.title: Producer prices (PPI final demand), YoY
ppi_yoy.note: Pipeline inflation — producer prices tend to move before consumer prices.

sloos.title: Banks tightening lending standards (C&I loans)
sloos.note: Senior Loan Officer Survey; credit tightening typically leads downturns by 2–3 quarters.

nfci.title: Financial conditions (Chicago Fed NFCI)
nfci.note: Positive = tighter than average financial conditions.

gpr.title: Geopolitical risk index (GPR)
gpr.note: News-based index of geopolitical tensions, monthly since 1985.

gscpi.title: Global supply chain pressure (GSCPI)
gscpi.note: Supply-chain stress shows up here before it shows up in goods prices.

approval.title: Presidential approval (poll average)
approval.note: Unweighted 14-day trailing mean of all public approval polls, stepped weekly.

midterm_odds.title: 2026 midterms: Democratic control odds (Polymarket)
midterm_odds.note: Prediction-market prices: 60 = a 60% implied probability of Democratic control of that chamber.

consumer_debt.title: Household debt balances
consumer_debt.note: What households owe outside their mortgage. Revolving is mostly credit cards; nonrevolving is mostly auto and student loans. Balances grow with the economy -- watch the pace, and read alongside the debt-service burden.

debt_burden.title: Household debt-service burden
debt_burden.note: Share of after-tax income households must put toward debt payments -- the cleanest read on whether debt loads are affordable. It climbed ahead of both the dot-com and 2008 downturns. Quarterly, since 1980.

violent_crime.title: Violent crime rate
violent_crime.note: Reported violent crimes per 100,000 people. Curated from FBI Uniform Crime Reporting; updated each fall.

property_crime.title: Property crime rate
property_crime.note: Reported property crimes (burglary, theft, motor-vehicle theft) per 100,000 people. Curated from FBI UCR; updated each fall.

homicide_clearance.title: Homicide clearance rate
homicide_clearance.note: Share of murders cleared by arrest or exceptional means. Curated from FBI UCR (consistent MAP series); updated each fall.

incarceration.title: Imprisonment rate
incarceration.note: Sentenced state and federal prisoners per 100,000 U.S. residents (excludes local jails). Curated from BJS.

race_ethnicity.title: Population by race & ethnicity (share)
race_ethnicity.note: Each group as a share of total U.S. population; Hispanic is an ethnicity counted separately from race, so shares do not sum to 100. Census decennial + latest estimates. 1970 figures are sample-based and pre-2000 Asian includes Pacific Islanders.

foreign_born.title: Foreign-born share of population
foreign_born.note: Share of U.S. residents born outside the country. The 2024 level (~14.8%) essentially ties the all-time high of 14.8% set in 1890.

pop_growth.title: Population growth rate
pop_growth.note: Annual U.S. population growth. It has trended down for decades toward record lows, and is now driven mostly by immigration rather than births.

natural_increase.title: Natural increase (births minus deaths)
natural_increase.note: U.S. births minus deaths per year, in millions. It collapsed from about 2 million in 1990 toward 0.2 million in 2021 as births fell and deaths rose - the reason population growth now hinges on immigration.

age_65plus.title: Share of population age 65+
age_65plus.note: Americans 65+ as a share of the population - roughly 10% in 1970 to about 18% now as the Baby Boomers age. Drives entitlement costs and the shrinking worker-to-retiree ratio.

naep_reading.title: Reading scores (NAEP), ages 9 & 13
naep_reading.note: NAEP Long-Term Trend reading scale scores (0-500) - actual test results, not attainment. Both ages slid after 2012, with the largest drops among lower-performing students.

naep_math.title: Math scores (NAEP), ages 9 & 13
naep_math.note: NAEP Long-Term Trend math scale scores (0-500). Age-13 math fell 14 points from its 2012 peak to 2023, back to 1990s levels.

chronic_absenteeism.title: Chronic absenteeism
chronic_absenteeism.source_name: U.S. Dept. of Education / AEI Return to Learn
chronic_absenteeism.note: Share of public-school students missing at least 10% of school days. It nearly doubled to about 28% after COVID (2021-22) and is receding only slowly.

pisa_math.title: Math: U.S. vs. OECD (PISA)
pisa_math.note: Mean math score of 15-year-olds, every three years. U.S. 2022 (465) was its lowest ever; it now sits near the OECD average largely because the OECD average itself fell.

border_monthly.title: Border encounters by month (nationwide vs. Southwest)
border_monthly.note: All CBP encounters per month — USBP Title 8 apprehensions + OFO Title 8 inadmissibles + Title 42 expulsions (the latter through May 2023) — from CBP's published Nationwide Encounters dataset, summed across demographics, citizenship and authority. Nationwide covers every sector and port; Southwest is the Southwest-land-border subset. Auto-discovers the newest monthly file and merges the FY archives back to FY2020; CBP posts on a roughly 4-6 week lag.
